mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers the equity discount rate and supports higher S&P 500 prices over the next month. The signal is strongest when CPIAUCSL is not accelerating, because disinflation preserves the probability of continued easing.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of periods when FEDFUNDS falls over a 3-month window and CPIAUCSL inflation is flat or declining, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent level signals labor-market deterioration, weaker household income growth, and softer risk appetite, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations deliver non-negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases or if the average triggered return is not below the unconditional mean by at least 1 percentage point.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in RSAFS indicates improving retail demand and revenue growth expectations, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the following week through better near-term earnings revisions.
failure_condition: Reject if the sign of the 5-day forward return association with positive RSAFS changes is negative for two consecutive rolling 1-year windows or if hit rate falls below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized rise in INDPRO and PAYEMS reflects broad production and employment expansion, which reduces recession risk and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if joint upturns in INDPRO and PAYEMS fail to beat the baseline 60-day return distribution, or if the conditional mean return is not positive over any rolling 3-year test window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST below its recent trend signals housing-demand weakening and tighter cyclical growth expectations, which should lead S&P 500 returns lower over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are positive in at least 55% of periods after the HOUST YoY decline trigger, or if the average conditional return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL rises while FEDFUNDS stays low, real rates fall and policy remains behind inflation, which should support equity multiples and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains below 3%
failure_condition: Reject if the triggered state is followed by negative or flat 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of occurrences, or if the mean return is not above the unconditional benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: A low and stable UNRATE indicates resilient income growth and reduced recession probability, which should keep S&P 500 returns positive over the next quarter unless employment growth is concurrently weakening.
failure_condition: Reject if low-UNRATE periods do not outperform overall 60-day returns, or if the signal disappears when PAYEMS growth is positive but UNRATE is falling.

mechanism_narrative: A weakening GDPC1 trend with stagnant RSAFS indicates broad demand deceleration, which should pressure earnings expectations and reduce S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 contracts q/q and RSAFS growth is below 0%
failure_condition: Reject if concurrent GDP contraction and weak retail sales do not predict negative 20-day SPX returns more often than chance, or if the mean conditional return is not below zero.
